title: create pod with mounts fails via rest api
body:
### Issue Description

Trying to create a pod with mounts using the rest api (via curl  or  podman-py).
Creation failed with `failed to make pod: container directory cannot be empty`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

This works.
1. podman volume create test
2. podman pod create --name test -v test:/mnt


Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman pod rm test
2. `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock -d '{"name":"test", "mounts":[{"type":"volume","source":"test","target":"/mnt"}]}' http://d/v4.0.0/libpod/pods/create`

`"cause":"container directory cannot be empty","message":"failed to make pod: container directory cannot be empty","response":500}`




### Describe the results you received

failed to make pod: container directory cannot be empty

### Describe the results you expected

pod is created with mounts

### Additional information

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
